description: Publishing a build artifact whose integrity, signature, or source provenance was never verified before release.
body: Failure mode: an artifact is pushed to a registry or deploy target without checking its checksum, signature, or that it was built from the reviewed tagged commit, so a corrupted, tampered, or stale build can ship. Detection signals: no checksum/signature recorded; artifact source commit cannot be confirmed; the deployed hash differs from the build output. Do not publish an artifact you have not verified end to end. Safer fallback: verify checksum and signature, confirm the artifact maps to the tagged source commit, and record the provenance before publishing. Risk boundary: if integrity or provenance cannot be confirmed, deny the publish and escalate rather than shipping an unverified artifact.
